words.def: to leave somebody, especially somebody you are responsible for, with no intention of returning
words.example: abandon somebody, The baby had been abandoned by its mother.

words.def: total and complete
words.example: I've joined a class for absolute beginners.

words.def: to take in a liquid, gas or other substance from the surface or space around
words.example: absorb something, Plants absorb carbon dioxide from the air.

words.def: based on general ideas and not on any particular real person, thing or situation
words.example: abstract knowledge/principles

words.def: connected with education, especially studying in schools and universities
words.example: high/low academic standards

words.def: a way of pronouncing the words of a language that shows which country, area or social class a person comes from; how well somebody pronounces a particular language
words.example: a northern/Dublin/Scottish accent

words.def: agreed or approved of by most people in a society
words.example: Children must learn socially acceptable behaviour.

words.def: by chance; in a way that was not planned
words.example: As I turned around, I accidentally hit him in the face.

words.def: to provide somebody with a room or place to sleep, live or sit
words.example: The hotel can accommodate up to 500 guests.

words.def: a place to live, work or stay in
words.example: rented/temporary accommodation

words.def: to travel or go somewhere with somebody/something
words.example: accompany somebody/something + adv./prep., His wife accompanied him on the trip.

words.def: to succeed in doing or completing something
words.example: The first part of the plan has been safely accomplished.

words.def: an arrangement that somebody has with a bank, etc. to keep money there, take some out, etc.
words.example: I don't have a bank account.

words.def: a person whose job is to keep or check financial accounts
words.example: We talked to the company’s chief accountant.

words.def: the state of being exact or correct; the ability to do something with skill and without making mistakes
words.example: They questioned the accuracy of the information in the file.

words.def: correct and true in every detail
words.example: an accurate description/picture of something

words.def: in a way that is correct and true in every detail
words.example: The article accurately reflects public opinion.

words.def: to say that somebody has done something wrong or is guilty of something
words.example: accuse somebody of something, to accuse somebody of murder/a crime

words.def: to accept that something is true
words.example: acknowledge something, She refuses to acknowledge the need for reform.

words.def: to gain something by your own efforts, ability or behaviour
words.example: She has acquired a good knowledge of English.

words.def: to make something such as a device or chemical process start working
words.example: The burglar alarm is activated by movement.

words.def: used to emphasize something that is real or exists in fact
words.example: James looks younger than his wife but in actual fact (= really) he is five years older.

words.def: to change your behaviour in order to deal more successfully with a new situation
words.example: It's amazing how soon you adapt.

words.def: the condition of being unable to stop using or doing something as a habit, especially something harmful
words.example: cocaine addiction

words.def: more than was first mentioned or is usual
words.example: additional resources/funds/security/funding/costs

words.def: in a way that is more than was first mentioned or is usual
words.example: Additionally, the bus service will run on Sundays, every two hours.

words.def: details of where somebody lives or works and where letters, etc. can be sent
words.example: What's your name and address?

words.def: enough in quantity, or good enough in quality, for a particular purpose or need
words.example: They'll need an adequate supply of hot water.

words.def: in a way that is enough in quantity, or good enough in quality, for a particular purpose or need
words.example: Are you adequately insured?

words.def: to change something slightly to make it more suitable for a new set of conditions or to make it work better
words.example: adjust something, Watch out for sharp bends and adjust your speed accordingly.

words.def: the activities that are done in order to plan, organize and run a business, school or other institution
words.example: Administration costs are passed on to the customer.

words.def: to take somebody else’s child into your family and become its legal parent(s)
words.example: a campaign to encourage childless couples to adopt

words.def: done or given before something is going to happen
words.example: Please give us advance warning of any changes.

words.def: events that are of public interest or political importance
words.example: world/international affairs

words.def: cheap enough that people can afford to pay it or buy it
words.example: We offer quality products at affordable prices.

words.def: at a later time; after an event that has already been mentioned
words.example: Afterwards she was sorry for what she'd said.

words.def: a business or an organization that provides a particular service especially when representing other businesses or organizations
words.example: She works for an advertising agency.

words.def: a list of items to be discussed at a meeting
words.example: We have a very full agenda of issues to discuss.

words.def: angry, and behaving in a threatening way; ready to attack
words.example: Seals have been known to exhibit aggressive behaviour towards swimmers.

words.def: the science or practice of farming
words.example: The number of people employed in agriculture has fallen in the last decade.

words.def: money, food, etc. that is sent to help countries in difficult situations
words.example: humanitarian/food/medical aid

words.def: a serious illness caused by a virus called HIV that attacks the body’s ability to resist infection (the abbreviation for ‘Acquired Immune Deficiency Syndrome’)
words.example: the AIDS epidemic

words.def: any vehicle that can fly and carry goods or passengers
words.example: fighter/transport/military aircraft

words.def: a loud noise or a signal that warns people of danger or of a problem
words.example: She decided to sound the alarm (= warn people that the situation was dangerous).

words.def: next to or at the side of something
words.example: A police car pulled up alongside us.

words.def: to become different; to make somebody/something different
words.example: Prices did not alter significantly during 2019.

words.def: (used to emphasize something) completely; in every way
words.example: The train went slower and slower until it stopped altogether.

words.def: a vehicle with special equipment, used for taking sick or injured people to a hospital
words.example: Call an ambulance!

words.def: a quantity of something
words.example: I was amazed at the amount he could eat.

words.def: funny and giving pleasure
words.example: an amusing story/game/incident

words.def: a person whose job involves examining facts or materials in order to give an opinion on them
words.example: a political/financial analyst

words.def: a person in your family who lived a long time ago
words.example: His ancestors had come to America from Ireland.

words.def: the strong feeling that you have when something has happened that you think is bad and unfair
words.example: She had to find a way to express her pent-up anger.

words.def: the space between two lines or surfaces that join, measured in degrees
words.example: a 45° angle

words.def: the process of making films, videos and computer games in which drawings, models or images of people and animals seem to move
words.example: computer/cartoon animation

words.def: a date that is an exact number of years after the date of an important or special event
words.example: on the anniversary of his wife’s death

words.def: happening or done once every year
words.example: an annual meeting/event/report/conference

words.def: once a year
words.example: The exhibition is held annually.

words.def: to expect something
words.example: anticipate something, We don't anticipate any major problems.
